ddls:
CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, developer, technical writer) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for secure authentication */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Flag to indicate if the user account is active */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the user's last login */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user account was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions associated with the role (e.g., read, write, delete) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Flag to indicate if the role is active */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the role was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the role was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "user_role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user-role mapping */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role */,
  "assigned_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who assigned the role */,
  "assigned_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the role was assigned */,
  "revoked_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the role was revoked (if applicable) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_role_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_roles_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES roles ("role_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_roles_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "service_versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each version */,
  "service_id" INTEGER /* ID of the service */,
  "version_number" TEXT /* Version number */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Date the version was released */,
  "changes" TEXT /* Changes made in this version */,
  "is_current" INTEGER /* Flag to indicate if the version is the current version */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the version was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the version was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "service_aliases" (
  "alias_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alias */,
  "service_id" INTEGER /* ID of the service */,
  "alias_name" TEXT /* Name of the alias */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the alias was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the alias was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alias_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "service_packages" (
  "package_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each package */,
  "service_id" INTEGER /* ID of the service */,
  "package_name" TEXT /* Name of the package */,
  "package_type" TEXT /* Type of the package (e.g., Go V1, Go V2) */,
  "package_version" TEXT /* Version of the package */,
  "package_url" TEXT /* URL to the package documentation or source code */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the package was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the package was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("package_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "service_resources" (
  "resource_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each resource */,
  "service_id" INTEGER /* ID of the service */,
  "resource_prefix_actual" TEXT /* Actual resource prefix */,
  "resource_prefix_correct" TEXT /* Correct resource prefix */,
  "resource_type" TEXT /* Type of the resource (e.g., S3 bucket, EC2 instance) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the resource was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the resource was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("resource_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "service_files" (
  "file_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each file */,
  "service_id" INTEGER /* ID of the service */,
  "file_prefix" TEXT /* File prefix for the service */,
  "file_type" TEXT /* Type of the file (e.g., configuration, script) */,
  "file_path" TEXT /* Path to the file */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the file was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the file was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("file_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "service_documents" (
  "doc_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each document */,
  "service_id" INTEGER /* ID of the service */,
  "doc_prefix" TEXT /* Documentation prefix for the service */,
  "document_type" TEXT /* Type of the document (e.g., API reference, user guide) */,
  "document_url" TEXT /* URL to the document */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the document was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the document was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("doc_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "service_env_vars" (
  "env_var_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each environment variable */,
  "service_id" INTEGER /* ID of the service */,
  "deprecated_env_var" TEXT /* Deprecated environment variable */,
  "env_var" TEXT /* Current environment variable */,
  "env_var_type" TEXT /* Type of the environment variable (e.g., API key, secret) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the environment variable was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the environment variable was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("env_var_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "service_notes" (
  "note_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each note */,
  "service_id" INTEGER /* ID of the service */,
  "note" TEXT /* Additional notes about the service */,
  "note_type" TEXT /* Type of the note (e.g., technical, operational) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the note was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the note was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("note_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "audit_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who performed the action */,
  "action" TEXT /* Action performed (e.g., view, update, delete) */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the action */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the action was performed */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the client performing the action */,
  "action_details" TEXT /* Detailed information about the action performed */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the log entry was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audit_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
